Skyfall (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $304,360,277Details
International Box Office $806,166,704
Worldwide Box Office $1,110,526,981
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $48,156,667
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $53,051,541
Total $101,208,208
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Bond's loyalty to M is tested as her past comes back to haunt her. As MI6 comes under attack, 007 must track down and destroy the threat, no matter how personal the cost

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$88,364,714 (29.0% of total gross)
Legs:3.44 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:27.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$200,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,505 opening theaters/3,526 max. theaters, 8.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $456,882,381

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 8th, 2012 (IMAX) by Sony Pictures
November 9th, 2012 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: October 26th, 2012 (Wide) (Jordan)
October 26th, 2012 (Wide) (Finland)
October 26th, 2012 (Wide) (Norway)
October 26th, 2012 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
October 26th, 2012 (Wide) (United Arab Emirates)
Video Release: February 12th, 2013 by Fox/MGM
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense violent sequences throughout, some sexuality, language and smoking.
(Rating bulletin 2242, 9/26/2012)
Running Time: 143 minutes
Franchise: James Bond
Cast, crew, or production detail: Development Hell, Intertitle, Legacy Sequel, Prologue
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Istanbul, London, Macau, Shanghai
Plot point: Faked Death, Secret Agent, Surprise Twist
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Eon Productions, B23, Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Pictures, Columbia Pictures
Production Countries: United Kingdom, United States
Languages: English
